elements: dotted arrows moving from bottom to top and top to bottom

interactions.teaches: Orbiting the cube helps you understand the spatial relationship between layers and arrows, illustrating the dual nature of representation and generative modeling.
Hover over the arrows to highlight the direction of transformation, emphasizing the role of arrows in indicating learning paths.

demo_steps.title: Cube Orientation
demo_steps.narration: The 3D cube is set up to highlight the flow from data, represented by layers at the bottom, to embedding, the abstract layer at the top. The dotted arrows, like dotted lines, signify the abstract processes taking place.

demo_steps.title: Representation Learning Flow
demo_steps.narration: Observe the transition from data to embedding through upward dotted arrows. Each layer adds abstraction, illustrating how data morphs into increasingly abstract forms.

demo_steps.title: Generative Modeling Flow
demo_steps.narration: Now, trace the same arrows downwards to see generative modeling in action. This reverse path shows how embedding resolves back into concrete data forms. This dual perspective enriches your understanding of deep nets.

demo_steps.title: Interconnected Learning
demo_steps.narration: Both pathways are interconnected, emphasizing the reciprocal nature of representation learning and generative modeling. This interplay is central to how we approach deep learning.

plan.contextChunk:
are, essentially, inverses of each other. Many algorithms explicitly
treat them as inverse problems, where solving the problem in one
direction can inform the solution in the other direction.

In @sec-neural_nets, we described neural nets as being a
sequence of mappings from raw data to ever more abstracted
representations, layer by layer. This perspective puts representation
learning in the spotlight: deep learning is just representation
learning! Let us now point out an alternative perspective: in backward
order, deep nets are mappings from abstracted representations to ever
more concrete representations of the data, layer by layer. This backward
ordering is the direction in which deep generative networks work. This
perspective puts the spotlight on generative modeling: deep learning is
just generative modeling! Indeed both modeling directions are valid, and
the full picture looks like this
(@fig-generative_modeling_and_representation_learning-rep_gen_schematic):

![The relationship between representation learning and generative modeling. Here we label one side as "Data" and the other as "Embedding"; but what's the precise difference between these two things? Why is an RGB image data while a 100-dimensional vector of neural activations is an embedding? This is a question for you to think about; there is no correct answer.](./figures/generative_modeling_and_representation_learning/rep_gen_schematic.png){width="35%" #fig-generative_modeling_and_representation_learning-rep_gen_schematic}

:::{.column-margin}
Moving backward through a net is also what backpropagation does, but it computes a different function: backpropagation computes the gradient $\nabla f$ whereas here we focus on the inverse $f^{-1}$.
:::



## Latent Variables as Representations

In @sec-generative_models, we introduced generative models
with latent variables $\mathbf{z}$. In that context, the role of the
latent variable was to specify all unobserved factors that might affect
the output of a model. For example, if the model predicts the color of a
black and white photo, it is a mapping
$g: \mathbf{x}, \mathbf{z} \rightarrow \mathbf{y}$, with $\mathbf{x}$

elements: dotted arrows moving from bottom to top

interactions.teaches: Allows you to explore how the layers and arrows relate spatially, reinforcing the dual perspective of representation and generative processes.

demo_steps.title: Overview of the Model
demo_steps.narration: Here, you see a transparent 3D cube with layers inside, symbolizing representations at different stages. The arrows show the flow from data to embedding (representation learning) and vice-versa (generative modeling).

demo_steps.title: Representation Learning Path
demo_steps.narration: Focus on the arrows moving upwards. This direction depicts the transformation of raw data into abstract representations, layer by layer, which is the essence of representation learning.

demo_steps.title: Generative Modeling Path
demo_steps.narration: Now, observe the same arrows as indicating generative modeling when viewed going downwards. This highlights how high-level abstract representations can generate concrete data outputs.

demo_steps.title: Connecting the Concepts
demo_steps.narration: Both learning paths are interconnected, illustrating how each concept informs the other. This interplay is vital for understanding deep learning structures.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Initial view is a slightly elevated perspective to show depth, layers, and arrows clearly.
interactionPlan.notes: Ensure the transparency of cube faces to maintain the visibility of all arrows and layers. Use subtle lighting to enhance 3D appearance.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The cube is rotated differently, causing perspective mismatch.
The arrows are lines without dotted texture.
The text labels' orientations and positions are incorrect.
The layering inside the cube lacks the correct transparency and is misaligned.
The overall color tone and opacity differ significantly.
evaluation.notes: The representation is recognizable but lacks precise alignment, color matching, and perspective of the original.
action_items: Adjust the cube rotation for accurate perspective.
Implement correct dashed arrow styles.
Reposition and reorient labels to match the source image.

feedback.rationale: The issues like incorrect cube rotation, dashed arrow styles, and label positioning are specific non-conceptual execution flaws that require generator refinement, not planning changes.
action_items: Adjust the cube rotation for accurate perspective.
Implement correct dashed arrow styles.
Reposition and reorient labels to match the source image.
